description_long: This object consists of two knives with long, slender blades and light-colored wooden handles. The blades are straight and taper to a sharp point, with one blade appearing to be made of polished metal and the other of a lighter, possibly stone or ceramic material with visible marbling. The handles are cylindrical, slightly contoured for grip, and display a natural wood grain pattern with a smooth finish. There are no visible guards or crossguards separating the blade from the handle. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on functionality. These knives are suitable for precise cutting tasks and would be commonly found in kitchen or food preparation settings.
description: A pair of long, slender knives with light wooden handles and straight, pointed blades—one metallic, one marbled stone or ceramic. The design is simple, functional, and unadorned.
description_view_0: Side view showing both knives in full length, highlighting the straight blades and light wooden handles.
description_view_1: Edge-on view displaying the thickness of the blades and the contoured shape of the handles.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again showing the full length of the knives and the marbled pattern on one blade.
description_view_3: Reverse edge-on view, emphasizing the handle ends and the blade profiles.
attribute_handle_design: cylindrical, contoured, light wood, smooth finish, visible wood grain
